title: Musical DNA: Computational Fingerprinting of Compositional Style and Its Application to Music Copyright Analysis (software and dataset)
description: Source code, copyright-case dataset, composer feature matrix, and interactive dashboard for the study 'Musical DNA'. Component A fingerprints classical-composer style from MIDI using 21 melodic, harmonic, rhythmic, and structural features (Random Forest, 88.7% six-composer test accuracy). Component B applies pairwise melodic, harmonic, rhythmic, and n-gram similarity metrics to a hand-curated dataset of 43 real music copyright cases (1946-2023) and finds that non-musical factors (plaintiff fame, defendant commercial success, expert testimony, litigation forum) predict case outcomes substantially better than musical similarity alone (AUC 0.86 vs. 0.39). Includes an open-source audio-to-MIDI similarity pipeline and a Streamlit dashboard. This archive accompanies the paper preprint (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21433890).
